mappings.rationale: Covers the structure of political systems including branches of government and types of political organization.
Covers democratic political systems including parliamentary rule and representative governance (subsections A.2 and B.1).
Covers a supranational political system and regional federation (subsection A.1.a).
Covers the legal framework of the EU as a supranational political system.
Covers the UN as a supranational political organization and world congress (subsection A.1.a).
Directly covers the federal state system and the distribution of power between levels of government (subsection A.1.b).
Covers the executive branch of government in a presidential system (subsection B.3).
Covers the legislature as a branch of government (subsection B.2).
Covers the judiciary as a branch of government (subsection B.4).
Covers the judicial branch and its role within the governmental system.
Covers the constitutional framework defining branches and offices of government.
Covers the structure and institutions of the American political system including its federal structure.
Covers parliamentary government and the concentration of legislative and executive functions (subsection B.1).
Covers the constitutional structure defining branches and offices in a parliamentary system.
Covers the structures and models of political systems and how they function in practice.

mappings.vsiAuthor: Stella Z. Theodoulou, Ravi K. Roy
mappings.rationale: Covers the administrative structures of government and how offices function within political systems.
Covers authoritarian political systems as a type and model of government (subsection A.2).
Covers empires as supranational political systems (subsection A.1.a).
Covers urban governments and the political organization of cities (subsection A.1.c).
Covers dynastic rule as a historical type of political system and model of government.
Covers the HRE as a historical example of a confederation and supranational political system.
Covers a multinational empire as a supranational political system.
Covers the British Empire as a supranational political system including commonwealths and dependencies.
Covers the Soviet system as a type of political system with concentrated executive and legislative power.
Covers foundational constitutional principles governing the structure of government.
Covers the Roman Republic as an early model of a political system with separated branches.
Covers the Roman Empire as a historical supranational political system.
